title: Add spec.resources field to Keystone CRD
description:
**Size:** 🔧 small
**Category:** backend
**Priority:** critical
**Source:** GitHub Issue [#120](https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/120)

Add an optional `spec.resources` field of type `*corev1.ResourceRequirements` to `KeystoneSpec` in `keystone_types.go` with `+optional` marker and `omitempty` JSON tag. In the defaulting webhook (`Default()` in `keystone_webhook.go`), inject sensible defaults (256Mi/512Mi memory, 100m/500m CPU) when `spec.Resources == nil`. In the validation webhook (`validateKeystone()`), reject specs where any resource request exceeds its corresponding limit via `specPath.Child("resources")` accumulated into `field.ErrorList`. Wire `spec.Resources` into the `keystone-api` container in `buildKeystoneDeployment()` in `reconcile_deployment.go`. Regenerate the CRD manifest via `controller-gen`. Add unit tests for webhook defaulting (nil → defaults, explicit preserved), validation (valid, request > limit rejected), and deployment builder (resources in container spec). Update CRD reference docs at `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md`.

**Rationale:** Without resource requests, pods run as BestEffort QoS and the existing HPA (CC-0038) cannot compute utilization ratios since it targets CPU/memory utilization percentages against undefined baselines. This is a correctness bug — HPA is effectively non-functional without defined resource requests to compute percentages against.

**Affected Areas:**
- operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go
- operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go
- operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook_test.go
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go
- helm/keystone-operator/crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml
- docs/reference/keystone-crd.md

stories.title: Keystone pods get sensible resource defaults
stories.want: Keystone pods to have resource requests and limits by default without me specifying them
stories.so_that: pods run as Burstable QoS class and HPA (CC-0038) can compute utilization ratios against defined baselines
criteria: Creating a Keystone CR without spec.resources results in pods with 256Mi/512Mi memory and 100m/500m CPU requests/limits
The defaulting webhook injects resources when spec.resources is nil
HPA targetCPUUtilization and targetMemoryUtilization can compute percentages against the defined resource requests
Existing Keystone CRs without the resources field continue to work after CRD upgrade

stories.title: Platform operator sets custom resource limits
stories.want: to specify custom CPU and memory requests/limits for Keystone pods
stories.so_that: I can right-size Keystone for my cluster's capacity and workload characteristics
criteria: Setting spec.resources with custom values preserves those values through the admission pipeline
The defaulting webhook does not overwrite explicitly provided resource values
The keystone-api container runs with the user-specified resources
Updating spec.resources on an existing CR triggers a rolling deployment update

stories.title: Invalid resource specs are rejected with clear errors
stories.want: to receive clear, field-specific error messages when I submit invalid resource configurations
stories.so_that: I can correct my CR spec without guessing what went wrong
criteria: Submitting CPU request > CPU limit returns error referencing spec.resources.requests.cpu
Submitting memory request > memory limit returns error referencing spec.resources.requests.memory
Both errors are reported simultaneously when both CPU and memory requests exceed limits
Valid resource specs where requests <= limits are accepted without error

stories.title: HPA computes utilization ratios correctly with resource baselines
stories.want: HPA to function correctly by computing CPU/memory utilization percentages against defined resource requests
stories.so_that: autoscaling responds to actual load rather than being non-functional due to undefined baselines
criteria: HPA targetCPUUtilization computes percentage against the container CPU request value
HPA targetMemoryUtilization computes percentage against the container memory request value
Default resource requests provide meaningful baselines for HPA calculations
Pods transition from BestEffort QoS to Burstable QoS class with defined resources

stories.title: CRD reference docs cover resources field comprehensively
stories.want: the CRD reference documentation to describe the resources field, its defaults, and validation rules
stories.so_that: I can understand how to configure resources without reading source code
criteria: KeystoneSpec table includes resources field with type *corev1.ResourceRequirements, required=No, and default values
Defaulting webhook table includes resources row with condition == nil and default values
Validation rules table includes the request-vs-limit rule with field path and error type
Resource Shape example YAML includes a resources section with sample values

requirements.description: The defaulting webhook SHALL inject resource defaults (256Mi request / 512Mi limit memory, 100m request / 500m limit CPU) when spec.resources is nil
requirements.rationale: Without resource requests, pods run as BestEffort QoS and HPA (CC-0038) cannot compute utilization ratios since it targets CPU/memory utilization percentages against undefined baselines. Defaults ensure every Keystone deployment has working resource management out of the box.

scenarios.name: Nil resources get defaults
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted without spec.resources (nil pointer)
scenarios.then: the defaulting webhook sets spec.resources to {requests: {memory: 256Mi, cpu: 100m}, limits: {memory: 512Mi, cpu: 500m}}
and_then: pods are created with Burstable QoS class

scenarios.name: Explicit resources preserved
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted with explicit spec.resources values (e.g. 1Gi/2Gi memory, 200m/1000m CPU)
scenarios.then: the defaulting webhook does not modify spec.resources
and_then: the user-specified values appear in the admitted CR unchanged

scenarios.name: Zero-value Keystone gets resources after Default()
scenarios.when: a zero-value Keystone CR passes through Default()
scenarios.then: spec.resources is non-nil with the documented defaults (256Mi/512Mi, 100m/500m)
and_then: all other zero-value defaults (replicas, cache.backend, etc.) are also applied

requirements.description: The validation webhook SHALL reject specs where any resource request exceeds its corresponding limit, accumulating all errors into field.ErrorList
requirements.rationale: Defense-in-depth validation catches request > limit at the CRD level before pods are created. The validation follows the existing field.ErrorList accumulation pattern (no short-circuiting) so operators see all errors at once.

scenarios.name: Valid resources accepted
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR has resource requests that are equal to or less than their corresponding limits

scenarios.name: CPU request exceeds limit rejected
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR has CPU request (e.g. 1000m) greater than CPU limit (e.g. 500m)
scenarios.then: validation returns a field.Invalid error at spec.resources.requests.cpu
and_then: error message states request must not exceed limit and includes the limit value

scenarios.name: Memory request exceeds limit rejected
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR has memory request (e.g. 1Gi) greater than memory limit (e.g. 256Mi)
scenarios.then: validation returns a field.Invalid error at spec.resources.requests.memory
and_then: error message states request must not exceed limit and includes the limit value

scenarios.name: Multiple violations reported simultaneously
scenarios.when: both CPU and memory requests exceed their corresponding limits
scenarios.then: both errors are accumulated in the field.ErrorList and returned together
and_then: the error response contains both spec.resources.requests.cpu and spec.resources.requests.memory paths

scenarios.name: Nil resources passes validation
scenarios.when: spec.resources is nil (e.g. before defaulting webhook runs or if webhook is bypassed)
scenarios.then: no resource-related validation errors are produced

scenarios.name: Requests without limits accepted
scenarios.when: spec.resources has only Requests set with no corresponding Limits entries
scenarios.then: validation passes because there are no limits to compare against

requirements.description: The buildKeystoneDeployment function SHALL include spec.resources in the keystone-api container specification
requirements.rationale: Resource requirements must flow from the CRD spec through the reconciler to the container spec for Kubernetes scheduling and HPA to function correctly.

scenarios.name: Default resources appear in container spec
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR with defaulted resources (256Mi/512Mi, 100m/500m) is reconciled
scenarios.then: the keystone-api container in the Deployment has Resources set to those default values
and_then: the container's Requests and Limits maps contain cpu and memory entries

scenarios.name: Custom resources appear in container spec
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR with custom resources (e.g. 1Gi/2Gi memory, 500m/2 CPU) is reconciled
scenarios.then: the keystone-api container has Resources set to the custom values from spec
and_then: a Deployment update is applied with the new resource values

scenarios.name: Nil resources handled safely
scenarios.when: buildKeystoneDeployment is called with a Keystone CR where spec.resources is nil (e.g. in tests without webhook)
scenarios.then: the function does not panic and the container has zero-value ResourceRequirements

requirements.description: The CRD manifest SHALL be regenerated to include the resources field with embedded corev1.ResourceRequirements schema, and synced to the Helm chart
requirements.rationale: The CRD manifest and DeepCopy must be regenerated so the Kubernetes API server recognizes the new field and the Helm chart stays in sync.

scenarios.name: CRD manifest includes resources field
scenarios.when: make manifests is run after adding the Resources field to KeystoneSpec
scenarios.then: the generated CRD YAML contains a resources property under spec with the ResourceRequirements OpenAPI v3 schema
and_then: the field is marked as optional in the schema

scenarios.name: Helm CRD stays in sync
scenarios.when: make sync-crds is run after regenerating the CRD manifest

scenarios.name: DeepCopy handles Resources pointer
scenarios.when: make generate is run after adding the *corev1.ResourceRequirements pointer field
scenarios.then: zz_generated.deepcopy.go includes nil-check and deep copy logic for the Resources pointer field

requirements.description: The CRD reference documentation SHALL include the resources field in the KeystoneSpec table, defaulting webhook table, and validation rules table
requirements.rationale: Platform engineers need to know how to configure resources, what defaults are applied, and what validation rules exist without reading source code.

scenarios.name: KeystoneSpec table includes resources
scenarios.when: a platform engineer reads the KeystoneSpec section of docs/reference/keystone-crd.md
scenarios.then: the table includes a resources row with type *corev1.ResourceRequirements, required No, default 256Mi/512Mi memory + 100m/500m CPU, and a description

scenarios.name: Webhook tables updated
scenarios.when: a platform engineer reads the Webhooks section
scenarios.then: the defaulting table includes resources (condition: == nil, default values) and the validation table includes the request-vs-limit rule

scenarios.name: Example YAML includes resources
scenarios.when: a platform engineer reads the Resource Shape example
scenarios.then: the example YAML includes a resources section with sample values

tasks.title: Add Resources field to KeystoneSpec and regenerate DeepCopy (CC-0042, REQ-004)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go: (1) Add import corev1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1". (2) Add Resources field to KeystoneSpec after NetworkPolicy: `// Resources defines resource requests and limits for the Keystone API container.\n// +optional\nResources *corev1.ResourceRequirements \`json:"resources,omitempty"\``. (3) Run `make generate` to regenerate zz_generated.deepcopy.go with the new pointer field.

tasks.title: Add resource defaulting in Default() webhook with unit tests (CC-0042, REQ-001)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go: (1) Add imports corev1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1" and "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/resource". (2) In Default(), add nil-check block after existing defaults: if obj.Spec.Resources == nil, set to &corev1.ResourceRequirements{Requests: {memory: 256Mi, cpu: 100m}, Limits: {memory: 512Mi, cpu: 500m}}. In keystone_webhook_test.go: (3) Add TestDefault_ResourcesSetWhenNil verifying nil → default injection with exact values. (4) Add TestDefault_ResourcesPreservedWhenExplicit verifying custom values (1Gi/2Gi, 200m/1) are not overwritten. (5) Update TestDefault_SetsZeroValueDefaults to also assert Resources is non-nil with defaults.

tasks.title: Add resource validation in validate() webhook with unit tests (CC-0042, REQ-002)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go validate(): (1) After the networkPolicy validation block, add: if k.Spec.Resources != nil, iterate over k.Spec.Resources.Requests; for each resourceName/request, check if Limits has the same key; if request.Cmp(limit) > 0, append field.Invalid at specPath.Child("resources", "requests", string(resourceName)) with message fmt.Sprintf("request must not exceed limit (%s)", limit.String()). In keystone_webhook_test.go: (2) TestValidate_ResourcesValidAccepted — valid requests <= limits passes. (3) TestValidate_ResourcesCPURequestExceedsLimitRejected — CPU 1000m > 500m rejected with error containing 'resources' and 'cpu'. (4) TestValidate_ResourcesMemoryRequestExceedsLimitRejected — memory 1Gi > 256Mi rejected with error containing 'resources' and 'memory'. (5) TestValidate_ResourcesBothExceedReportsBothErrors — both exceed → error contains both 'cpu' and 'memory'. (6) TestValidate_ResourcesNilAccepted — nil Resources → no error. (7) TestValidate_ResourcesRequestsOnlyAccepted — Requests without Limits → no error.

tasks.title: Wire resources into buildKeystoneDeployment with unit tests (CC-0042, REQ-003)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go buildKeystoneDeployment(): (1) Before the return statement, declare `var resources corev1.ResourceRequirements; if keystone.Spec.Resources != nil { resources = *keystone.Spec.Resources }`. (2) Add `Resources: resources` to the keystone-api container struct literal. In reconcile_deployment_test.go: (3) Update deployTestKeystone() to include default Resources (256Mi/512Mi, 100m/500m). (4) Add imports for "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/resource". (5) Add TestReconcileDeployment_ContainerResources verifying the container has correct default resources (256Mi request, 512Mi limit, 100m request, 500m limit). (6) Add TestReconcileDeployment_CustomResources with custom Keystone (1Gi/2Gi, 500m/2) verifying custom resources appear in container.

tasks.title: Regenerate CRD manifest and sync to Helm chart (CC-0042, REQ-004)
tasks.description: Run `make manifests` to regenerate operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml with the new resources field schema. Run `make sync-crds` to copy the regenerated CRD to helm/keystone-operator/crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml with the sync header comment. Run `make verify-crd-sync` to verify the Helm CRD matches the generated source.

tasks.title: Add integration tests for resources field admission pipeline (CC-0042, REQ-001, REQ-002)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/integration_test.go, add three tests with //go:build integration tag: (1) TestIntegration_ResourcesDefaultedWhenNil — create valid CR without resources, Get it back, verify spec.resources has 256Mi/512Mi/100m/500m defaults. (2) TestIntegration_ResourcesPreservedWhenExplicit — create valid CR with custom resources (1Gi/2Gi, 200m/1), Get it back, verify values preserved. (3) TestIntegration_ResourcesRequestExceedsLimitRejected — create CR with CPU request 1000m > limit 500m, expect apierrors.IsInvalid or IsForbidden with error containing 'resources'. Follow existing integration test patterns: use testutil.SkipIfEnvTestUnavailable(t), construct CR from integrationValidKeystone() helper, use unique names per test.

tasks.title: Update CRD reference documentation for resources field (CC-0042, REQ-005)
tasks.description: In docs/reference/keystone-crd.md: (1) Add CC-0042 to feature header. (2) Add resources row to KeystoneSpec table: `| \`resources\` | [\`*corev1.ResourceRequirements\`](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/api/core/v1#ResourceRequirements) | No | 256Mi/512Mi memory, 100m/500m CPU | Resource requests and limits for the Keystone API container. When nil, sensible defaults are injected by the webhook. |`. (3) Add resources to the Resource Shape example YAML with sample values. (4) Add row to Defaulting Webhook table: `| \`spec.resources\` | \`== nil\` | \`{requests: {memory: 256Mi, cpu: 100m}, limits: {memory: 512Mi, cpu: 500m}}\` |`. (5) Add row to Validation Rules table: `| Resource request exceeds limit | \`spec.resources.requests.<type>\` | \`field.Invalid\` | Any resource request value exceeds its corresponding limit (checked for each resource type independently). |`. (6) Add CredentialKeysSpec row to Generated DeepCopy Types list if missing, and add NetworkPolicySpec + NetworkPolicyIngressSource.

test_specifications.story: Keystone pods get sensible resource defaults
test_specifications.expected: Default() on Keystone with nil spec.resources should set Resources to {Requests: {memory: 256Mi, cpu: 100m}, Limits: {memory: 512Mi, cpu: 500m}}

test_specifications.story: Platform operator sets custom resource limits
test_specifications.expected: Default() on Keystone with explicit Resources (1Gi/2Gi memory, 200m/1 CPU) should not modify the values

test_specifications.story: Invalid resource specs are rejected with clear errors
test_specifications.expected: Keystone with CPU request=100m/limit=500m and memory request=256Mi/limit=512Mi should pass validation without error

test_specifications.story: Invalid resource specs are rejected with clear errors
test_specifications.expected: Keystone with CPU request=1000m and limit=500m should fail validation with error containing 'resources' and 'cpu' and 'must not exceed limit'

test_specifications.story: Invalid resource specs are rejected with clear errors
test_specifications.expected: Keystone with memory request=1Gi and limit=256Mi should fail validation with error containing 'resources' and 'memory' and 'must not exceed limit'

test_specifications.story: Invalid resource specs are rejected with clear errors
test_specifications.expected: Keystone with both CPU (1000m > 500m) and memory (1Gi > 256Mi) request > limit should report both errors; error contains both 'cpu' and 'memory'

test_specifications.story: Invalid resource specs are rejected with clear errors
test_specifications.expected: Keystone with nil spec.resources should pass validation without any resource-related errors

test_specifications.story: Invalid resource specs are rejected with clear errors
test_specifications.expected: Keystone with only Requests set (cpu: 100m, memory: 256Mi, no Limits) should pass validation

test_specifications.story: Keystone pods get sensible resource defaults
test_specifications.expected: The keystone-api container in the built Deployment should have Resources with Requests {memory: 256Mi, cpu: 100m} and Limits {memory: 512Mi, cpu: 500m}

test_specifications.story: Platform operator sets custom resource limits
test_specifications.expected: The keystone-api container should have Resources matching custom spec values (e.g. 1Gi request, 2Gi limit memory) when set on the Keystone CR

test_specifications.story: Keystone pods get sensible resource defaults
test_specifications.expected: CR created without spec.resources should have defaults (256Mi/512Mi, 100m/500m) applied after passing through the full admission pipeline (mutating webhook → schema → validating webhook)

test_specifications.story: Platform operator sets custom resource limits
test_specifications.expected: CR created with explicit resources (1Gi/2Gi, 200m/1) should preserve those values unchanged after admission

test_specifications.story: Invalid resource specs are rejected with clear errors
test_specifications.expected: CR with CPU request=1000m > limit=500m should be rejected by the validating webhook with IsInvalid or IsForbidden error containing 'resources'

review_criteria: All SHALL requirements (REQ-001 through REQ-005) have corresponding passing tests — no untested code paths
Webhook defaulting sets exactly 256Mi/512Mi memory and 100m/500m CPU when spec.resources is nil, and does not modify non-nil values
Webhook validation iterates over Requests map and checks against corresponding Limits entries, accumulating all errors into field.ErrorList without short-circuiting
Field paths in validation errors use the established specPath.Child() pattern: spec.resources.requests.cpu, spec.resources.requests.memory
buildKeystoneDeployment safely handles nil spec.resources (no panic) and correctly wires non-nil resources into the keystone-api container
CRD manifest is regenerated (make manifests) and synced to Helm (make sync-crds); make verify-crd-sync passes
Integration tests verify the full admission pipeline: defaulting, preservation of explicit values, and rejection of invalid resources through envtest
Documentation includes resources field in KeystoneSpec table, Defaulting Webhook table, Validation Rules table, and Resource Shape example
No regressions: all existing unit tests (go test ./operators/keystone/...) and integration tests continue to pass
Code follows existing codebase patterns: pointer field with +optional marker, nil-check defaulting, field.ErrorList accumulation, defense-in-depth comments referencing CC-0042 and REQ-IDs
implementation_notes:
## Architecture

```
┌───────────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────────────────┐     ┌───────────────────────────────┐
│  KeystoneSpec         │     │  Defaulting Webhook          │     │  Validating Webhook           │
│  (keystone_types.go)  │────▶│  Default() in webhook.go     │────▶│  validate() in webhook.go     │
│                       │     │                              │     │                               │
│  Resources            │     │  if Resources == nil:         │     │  for each request in Requests: │
│  *corev1.Resource     │     │    inject defaults            │     │    if request > limit:         │
│   Requirements        │     │    (256Mi/512Mi, 100m/500m)  │     │      append field.Invalid      │
└───────────────────────┘     └──────────────────────────────┘     └───────────────────────────────┘
                                                                              │
                                                                              ▼
                              ┌──────────────────────────────┐     ┌───────────────────────────────┐
                              │  Deployment Reconciler       │     │  Kubernetes                   │
                              │  buildKeystoneDeployment()   │     │                               │
                              │  in reconcile_deployment.go  │     │  Scheduler: QoS-aware         │
                              │                              │────▶│  HPA: utilization %           │
                              │  Container.Resources =       │     │    = usage / request           │
                              │    *spec.Resources           │     │                               │
                              └──────────────────────────────┘     └───────────────────────────────┘
```

## Data Flow

1. User creates/updates Keystone CR (spec.resources may be nil or explicit)
2. Mutating webhook: Default() checks if Resources == nil → injects defaults
3. CRD schema validation: Kubernetes validates the ResourceRequirements structure
4. Validating webhook: validate() checks each request vs corresponding limit
5. Reconciler: buildKeystoneDeployment() reads spec.Resources and sets it on the container
6. Kubernetes scheduler uses resource requests for bin-packing decisions
7. HPA computes utilization ratios: current_usage / resource_request

## Key Design Decisions

1. **Pointer field pattern**: Resources is `*corev1.ResourceRequirements` (pointer) with `+optional` and `omitempty`, matching the established pattern used by Autoscaling, NetworkPolicy, and Federation fields on KeystoneSpec. Nil means 'not specified by user' which triggers defaulting.

2. **Nil-check defaulting**: The webhook uses `obj.Spec.Resources == nil` (not zero-value check) because ResourceRequirements has no natural zero value that distinguishes 'unset' from 'intentionally empty'. This differs from scalar defaults (replicas == 0) but matches the pointer-field convention.

3. **Generic resource iteration**: Validation iterates over `Requests` map keys rather than hardcoding CPU/memory, making it automatically handle any future resource types (e.g. ephemeral-storage, hugepages). The field path uses `string(resourceName)` for accurate error reporting.

4. **Safe nil handling in builder**: buildKeystoneDeployment handles nil Resources gracefully with a zero-value fallback, even though the webhook guarantees non-nil. This prevents panics if the reconciler processes a CR that bypassed the webhook (e.g. pre-existing CRs during operator upgrade).

## Import Requirements

- **keystone_types.go**: Add `corev1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"`
- **keystone_webhook.go**: Add `corev1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"` and `"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/resource"` (for MustParse in defaults)
- **keystone_webhook_test.go**: Already imports `corev1` — add `"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/resource"` for test assertions
- **reconcile_deployment_test.go**: Add `"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/resource"` for test helper

## Pitfalls

1. **DeepCopy generation**: Must run `make generate` after adding the pointer field to ensure zz_generated.deepcopy.go handles nil-check correctly. Forgetting this causes compilation errors.

2. **Test helper updates**: `deployTestKeystone()` in reconcile_deployment_test.go must include Resources to match what the webhook would produce. The `validKeystone()` helper in webhook tests does NOT need Resources (nil is valid for validation since the check is only triggered when Resources is non-nil).

3. **CRD sync**: Must run both `make manifests` AND `make sync-crds` — the first generates the CRD, the second copies it to the Helm chart directory. `make verify-crd-sync` in CI catches desync.

4. **Resource quantity comparison**: Use `request.Cmp(limit) > 0` (not `request > limit`) because resource.Quantity does not support Go comparison operators.
